steps.description: Press the F1 key in Visual Studio Code to open the command palette.
Type 'provision' into the VS Code command palette input field at the top of the screen.
Press Enter to select the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command in the VS Code command palette.
Click the "+ New resource group" option in the "Select a resource group" dropdown within Visual Studio Code.
Type #text:${{var:app_name}}-rg into the "New resource group name" input field in the Visual Studio Code "Provision" workflow dialog.
Press 'Enter' to confirm the resource group name in the "New resource group name" input field at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Select "Central US" from the "Location for the new resource group" dropdown in the Microsoft Visual Studio Code extension interface.
Click the "Provision" button in the confirmation dialog to approve provisioning resources in the dev environment using the listed Azure and Microsoft 365 accounts within Visual Studio Code.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type #text:'Notifications: Show Notifications' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top center of the window.
Press Enter to select "Notifications: Show Notifications" from the VS Code command palette.
